title: Architecture - automatedRecLin
text: automatedRecLin R package record linkage entity resolution. main abstractions comparison-vector construction, supervised unsupervised MEC model fitting, prediction trained models, blocked unsupervised linkage external blocking package. implementation organized small R package using data.table tabular data flow, reclin2 default comparators, nleqslv densityratio continuous estimation, FixedPoint fixed-point match-count calculations. blocked MEC path exposes alpha, nonmatch drop fraction used later U-side distribution fitting. first U-side fit remains uncorrected, posterior count formulas continue use full current nonmatch complement. Internal U-side fitting diagnostics remain available fitting helper, public mec_blocking() output keeps user-facing summaries.

title: Architecture - automatedRecLin
text: Thin exported entry points: Public functions validate user-facing arguments, normalize data data.table, delegate algorithmic work internal helpers. Shared comparison space: comparison_vectors() common bridge record data MEC inputs supervised fitting, unsupervised fitting, prediction, blocked candidate-space fitting. Candidate-space blocked MEC: mec_blocking() fits one pooled inverted MEC model blocked candidate-pair space rather sampled training blocks. U-side fitting separation: U-side fitting subset can smaller full current nonmatch complement, posterior count updates still use full complement size. Deterministic ranking selection: U-side retention ranks previous nonmatch reliability deterministic pair tie-breaking; final matches still use greedy one--one selection. Explicit blocked-MEC outputs: mec_blocking() returns alpha user-facing linkage, blocking, parameter, evaluation summaries, omitting internal fitting metadata. Evaluation optional post-processing: Metrics confusion matrices computed true_matches supplied. Direct checkout validation script: internal/test.R uses devtools::load_all(".", quiet = TRUE) direct script execution exercises working-tree implementation instead installed package.

title: Architecture - automatedRecLin
text: current mec_blocking() API keeps top-level alpha field omits internal fitting metadata initialization counts, U-fit diagnostics, ratio-orientation metadata, nested pooled model. first U-side fit records reason = "first_u_fit_full" apply alpha drop, even alpha > 0. Later U-side fits may record reason = "alpha_reliability_drop", alpha_zero, requested_drop_zero, base_smaller_than_requested_keep, minimum_sample_full_base. Initial inverted MEC selection now uses structural one--one bound nu directly. man/mec_blocking.Rd synchronized roxygen comments, inst/tinytest/test_mec_blocking.R asserts trimmed public output contract.

title: MEC with blocking
text: use full example Census Customer Information System (CIS) datasets McLeod et al. (2011). goal link records CIS records Census. person_id variable identifies correct linkage. use information evaluate result.
code: data("census", package = "automatedRecLin") data("cis", package = "automatedRecLin") setDT(census) setDT(cis)  NROW(cis) #> [1] 24613 NROW(census) #> [1] 25343 cis[is.na(cis)] <- "" census[is.na(census)] <- ""  cis[, pername1 := gsub("-", "", pername1)] census[, pername1 := gsub("-", "", pername1)]  true_matches <- merge(   x = cis[, .(a = .I, person_id)],   y = census[, .(b = .I, person_id)],   by = "person_id" )[, .(a, b)]  NROW(true_matches) #> [1] 24043

title: MEC with blocking
text: compare forename surname using Jaro-Winkler distance. two comparison variables modeled continuous parametric MEC method. Sex date--birth variables use default binary method. Address fields used construct blocks. Run blocked MEC. model trained candidate pairs retained blocking.
code: variables <- c(   "pername1", "pername2", "sex",   "dob_day", "dob_mon", "dob_year" )  comparators <- list(   "pername1" = jarowinkler_complement(),   "pername2" = jarowinkler_complement() )  methods <- list(   "pername1" = "continuous_parametric",   "pername2" = "continuous_parametric" )  blocking_variables <- c(variables, "enumcap", "enumpc") set.seed(1)  result <- mec_blocking(   A = cis,   B = census,   variables = variables,   comparators = comparators,   methods = methods,   blocking_variables = blocking_variables,   blocking_sep = " ",   controls_blocking = list(seed = 1, n_threads = 1),   alpha = 0.5,   true_matches = true_matches )  result #> Blocked MEC record linkage based on:   #> pername1, pername2, sex, dob_day, dob_mon, dob_year. #> ======================================================== #> The algorithm predicted 23697 matches. #> The first 6 predicted matches are: #>        a     b block ratio / 1000 #>    <int> <int> <num>        <num> #> 1: 12264 18361 17172 3.428838e-12 #> 2: 23367 13031 12223 3.428838e-12 #> 3: 23495 15194 14243 3.428838e-12 #> 4:  1768 12279 11529 7.032757e-12 #> 5:   343 18657 17446 8.007616e-12 #> 6:  2124  5497  5152 8.007616e-12 #> ======================================================== #> ======================================================== #> Blocking diagnostics: #> Known matches: 24043. #> Known matches retained by blocking: 23687. #> Known matches missed by blocking: 356. #> Blocking MMR: 1.4807 %. #> Candidate pairs retained: 25343 of 623767259. #> Candidate pair reduction: 99.9959 %. #> ======================================================== #> Evaluation metrics: #> FLR (%) MMR (%)  #>  0.0506  1.4890

title: Record Linkage Based on an Entropy-Maximizing Classifier
text: Generate two simple datasets contain common records, typos cases. Specify key variables used record linkage. Select comparison function (.e., function compare pairs records) variable. example, use jarowinkler_complement() automatedRecLin package (Jaro-Winkler distance, .e., 1 - Jaro-Winkler similarity). Choose method estimating probability density ratio variable. available methods : "binary", "continuous_parametric", "continuous_nonparametric", "hit_miss" (unsupervised learning). Perform record linkage using mec(). output contains following information: names key variables, number predicted matches, first 6 predicted matches (estimated probability density ratio), method constructing predicted set matches (default: "size"), estimated false link rate (FLR), estimated missing match rate (MMR), estimated parameters variables using "binary", "continuous_parametric" "hit_miss" methods.

what: Integration with a custom machine learning model
title: Record Linkage Based on an Entropy-Maximizing Classifier
text: automatedRecLin package supports supervised record linkage using custom machine learning (ML) model predicts probability matching based comparison vectors (e.g., XGBoost, logistic regression). example, install load xgboost package. Use data, variables, comparators previous example. First, use comparison_vectors() create comparison vectors model trained . Train XGBoost model. Create XGBoost-based record linkage model. Use model predictions. Note xgboost package requires matrix input predict() method rec_lin_model objects needs specified data_type argument. Set type = "response" ensure XGBoost model predicts probability matching (argument may vary depending model library used).
code: # install.packages("xgboost") # uncomment if needed library(xgboost) vectors <- comparison_vectors(A = df_1_train, B = df_2_train,                               variables = variables_train,                               comparators = comparators_train,                               matches = matches_train) vectors #> Comparison based on the following variables: name, surname. #> ======================================================== #>        a     b gamma_name gamma_surname match #>    <int> <int>      <num>         <num> <num> #> 1:     1     1  0.0000000     0.0000000     1 #> 2:     1     2  0.4777778     0.5523810     0 #> 3:     1     3  0.5523810     1.0000000     0 #> 4:     1     4  1.0000000     0.5444444     0 #> 5:     1     5  0.5629630     1.0000000     0 #> 6:     1     6  1.0000000     1.0000000     0 model_xgb <- xgboost(x = as.matrix(vectors$Omega[, c("gamma_name", "gamma_surname")]),                      y = factor(vectors$Omega$match),                      objective = "binary:logistic", eval_metric = "logloss",                      nrounds = 100, verbosity = 0, nthread = 1) custom_xgb_model <- custom_rec_lin_model(model_xgb, vectors) custom_xgb_model #> Record linkage model based on the following variables: name, surname. #> A custom ML model was used. #> The prior probability of matching is 0.04. #> ======================================================== #> Probability/density ratio type: 2. result_xgb <- predict(custom_xgb_model, df_1_new, df_2_new,                       data_type = "matrix", type = "response") result_xgb #> The algorithm predicted 3 matches. #> The first 3 predicted matches are: #>        a     b ratio / 1000 #>    <int> <int>        <num> #> 1:     1     1    0.0299477 title: Create a Custom Record Linkage Model — custom_rec_lin_model
code: if (requireNamespace("xgboost", quietly = TRUE)) {   df_1 <- data.frame(     "name" = c("James", "Emma", "William", "Olivia", "Thomas",     "Sophie", "Harry", "Amelia", "George", "Isabella"),     "surname" = c("Smith", "Johnson", "Brown", "Taylor", "Wilson",     "Davis", "Clark", "Harris", "Lewis", "Walker")   )   df_2 <- data.frame(     "name" = c("James", "Ema", "Wimliam", "Olivia", "Charlotte",     "Henry", "Lucy", "Edward", "Alice", "Jack"),     "surname" = c("Smith", "Johnson", "Bron", "Tailor", "Moore",     "Evans", "Hall", "Wright", "Green", "King")   )   comparators <- list("name" = jarowinkler_complement(),                       "surname" = jarowinkler_complement())   matches <- data.frame("a" = 1:4, "b" = 1:4)   vectors <- comparison_vectors(A = df_1, B = df_2, variables = c("name", "surname"),                                comparators = comparators, matches = matches)   model_xgb <- xgboost::xgboost(x = as.matrix(vectors$Omega[, c("gamma_name", "gamma_surname")]),                        y = factor(vectors$Omega$match),                        objective = "binary:logistic", eval_metric = "logloss",                        nrounds = 100, verbosity = 0, nthread = 1)   custom_xgb_model <- custom_rec_lin_model(model_xgb, vectors)   custom_xgb_model } #> Record linkage model based on the following variables: name, surname. #> A custom ML model was used. #> The prior probability of matching is 0.04. #> ======================================================== #> Probability/density ratio type: 2.

title: Unsupervised Maximum Entropy Classifier for Record Linkage — mec
text: Consider two datasets without duplicates: \(\) \(B\). Let bipartite comparison space \(\Omega = \times B\) consist matches \(M\) non-matches \(U\) records files \(\) \(B\). pair records \((,b) \\Omega\), let \(\pmb{\gamma}_{ab} = (\gamma_{ab}^1,\gamma_{ab}^2, \ldots,\gamma_{ab}^K)'\) comparison vector set key variables. original MEC algorithm uses binary comparison function evaluate record pairs across two datasets. However, approach may insufficient handling datasets frequent errors across multiple variables. propose use continuous comparison functions address limitations binary comparison methods. consider every semi-metric, .e., function \(d: \times B \\mathbb{R}\), satisfying following conditions: \(d(x,y) \geq 0\), \(d(x,y) = 0\) \(x = y\), \(d(x,y) = d(y,x)\). example, can use Jaro-Winkler distance character variables (implemented automatedRecLin package jarowinkler_complement()) Euclidean distance numerical variables. automatedRecLin package allows use different comparison function key variable (specified list comparators argument). default function key variable cmp_identical() (binary comparison function). mec() function offers different approaches estimate probability/density ratio matches non-matches, specified list methods argument. available methods suitable binary comparison function "binary" "hit_miss". assume \(\gamma_{ab}^k|M\) \(\gamma_{ab}^k|U\) follow Bernoulli distributions. "binary" "hit_miss" estimate parameters matches iteratively, "binary" estimates parameters non-matches start, "hit_miss" iteratively using hit-miss model (details see Lee et al. (2022)). "binary" default method variable. continuous semi-metrics suggest usage "continuous_parametric" "continuous_nonparametric" method. "continuous_parametric" method assumes \(\gamma_{ab}^k|M\) \(\gamma_{ab}^k|U\) follow hurdle Gamma distributions. density function hurdle Gamma distribution characterized three parameters \(p_0 \(0,1)\) \(\alpha, \beta > 0\) follows: $$ f(x;p_0,\alpha,\beta) = p_0^{\mathbb{}(x = 0)}[(1 - p_0) v(x;\alpha,\beta)]^{\mathbb{}(x > 0)}, $$ $$ v(x;\alpha,\beta) = \frac{\beta^{\alpha} x^{\alpha - 1} \exp(-\beta x)} {\Gamma(\alpha)} $$ density function Gamma distribution (details see Vo et al. (2023)). beginning, algorithm estimates parameters non-matches iteratively matches. "continuous_nonparametric" method assume anything distributions comparison vectors. iteratively directly estimates density ratio matches non-matches, using Kullback-Leibler Importance Estimation Procedure (KLIEP). details see Sugiyama et al. (2008). mec() function allows construction predicted set matches using estimated size bisection procedure, described Lee et al. (2022), based target false link rate (FLR) missing match rate (MMR). use second option, set set_construction = "flr" set_construction = "mmr" specify target error rate using target_rate argument. assumption \(\) \(B\) contain duplicate records might relaxed allowing \(\) duplicates. , set duplicates_in_A = TRUE.

title: Train a Record Linkage Model — train_rec_lin
text: Consider two datasets: \(\) \(B\). Let bipartite comparison space \(\Omega = \times B\) consist matches \(M\) non-matches \(U\) records files \(\) \(B\). pair records \((,b) \\Omega\), let \(\pmb{\gamma}_{ab} = (\gamma_{ab}^1,\gamma_{ab}^2, \ldots,\gamma_{ab}^K)'\) comparison vector set key variables. original MEC algorithm uses binary comparison function evaluate record pairs across two datasets. However, approach may insufficient handling datasets frequent errors across multiple variables. propose use continuous comparison functions address limitations binary comparison methods. consider every semi-metric, .e., function \(d: \times B \\mathbb{R}\), satisfying following conditions: \(d(x,y) \geq 0\), \(d(x,y) = 0\) \(x = y\), \(d(x,y) = d(y,x)\). example, can use Jaro-Winkler distance character variables (implemented automatedRecLin package jarowinkler_complement()) Euclidean distance numerical variables. automatedRecLin package allows use different comparison function key variable (specified list comparators argument). default function key variable cmp_identical() (binary comparison function). train_rec_lin() function used train record linkage model, \(M\) \(U\) known (might later serve classifier pairs outside \(\Omega\)). offers different approaches estimate probability/density ratio matches non-matches, specified list methods argument. method suitable binary comparison function "binary", also default method variable. continuous semi-metrics suggest usage "continuous_parametric" "continuous_nonparametric" method. "continuous_parametric" method assumes \(\gamma_{ab}^k|M\) \(\gamma_{ab}^k|U\) follow hurdle Gamma distributions. density function hurdle Gamma distribution characterized three parameters \(p_0 \(0,1)\) \(\alpha, \beta > 0\) follows: $$ f(x;p_0,\alpha,\beta) = p_0^{\mathbb{}(x = 0)}[(1 - p_0) v(x;\alpha,\beta)]^{\mathbb{}(x > 0)}, $$ $$ v(x;\alpha,\beta) = \frac{\beta^{\alpha} x^{\alpha - 1} \exp(-\beta x)} {\Gamma(\alpha)} $$ density function Gamma distribution (details see Vo et al. (2023)). "continuous_nonparametric" method assume anything distributions comparison vectors. directly estimates density ratio matches non-matches, using Kullback-Leibler Importance Estimation Procedure (KLIEP). details see Sugiyama et al. (2008).

title: Version 1.1.1
text: Improved mec_blocking() using inverted unsupervised MEC. Added alpha mec_blocking() controlling FLR-MMR trade-.

title: Version 1.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-08 Added mec_blocking() blocked unsupervised MEC pooled training blockwise prediction using blocking package. Added support creating comparison vectors supplied table record pairs pairs argument comparison_vectors(). Added census cis example datasets larger record linkage examples. Added vignette showing MEC blocking cis census datasets. Added optional progress messages via verbose argument mec(), train_rec_lin(), predict.rec_lin_model(), mec_blocking(). Improved validation supplied match pair tables, including clearer checks row indices, duplicate pairs, missing values, non-finite comparison values. Improved print methods linkage results, including consistent percentage formatting error rates.

title: Version 1.0.1
text: CRAN release: 2025-12-13 Fixed CRAN errors.

title: Version 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-11-18 Implemented comparison functions abs_distance() jarowinkler_complement(). Added support comparing two datasets using comparison functions. Added support training supervised record linkage model using probability density ratio estimation, based following methods: "binary", "continuous_parametric", "continuous_nonparametric". Added support creating supervised record linkage model using custom machine learning (ML) classifier. Added support predicting matches based record linkage model. Added unsupervised maximum entropy classification (MEC) algorithm record linkage. Supported methods : "binary", "continuous_parametric", "continuous_nonparametric", "hit_miss". Added support creating predicted set matches based : estimated size, target false link rate (FLR) target missing match rate (MMR). Implemented S3 methods printing. Added support evaluation true matches known. Added documentation examples.
